highlights: Combines file explorer, Monaco editor, and multiple terminals in a single browser workspace.
Uses hidden tmux-backed terminal persistence so sessions survive browser refreshes.
Adds practical bridges like drag-drop uploads that immediately pipe usable paths into terminal flow.
impact: Improved remote development ergonomics by reducing context switching across terminal, editor, and filesystem operations.
one_liner: Browser-native terminal IDE shell with persistent tmux sessions and integrated file/edit workflows.
problem: Remote CLI work is fragmented across separate tools, making iterative operational tasks slower and more error-prone.
solution: Built a web workspace that unifies terminal panes, editing, and filesystem APIs behind one low-friction operational interface.
title: Browser TUI
visibility_note: Core repository is private. This entry summarizes architecture and working approach without exposing proprietary source.
